what_it_does: Renders an HTML design-system preview card for a six-tier gold brand accent scale, with usage guidance, color chips, tier names, and hex values.
observations: Includes a @dsCard metadata comment with group "Brand", name "Gold scale", subtitle "Primary brand accent — 6 tiers", and viewport "700x200".
Loads shared styling from "_card.css" and adds file-local CSS for the card layout, lead copy, swatch grid, chips, labels, and hex text.
Presents six swatches: gold-50 #FBF6DC, gold-soft #F4ECC4, gold-dashed #D8C040, gold ★ #D8B820, gold-hover #C0A218, and gold-press #9C8413.
Uses CSS custom properties for fonts, foreground colors, border radius, and faint border color.
States that --accent-gold is the only loud color in the system and should be reserved for primary actions, active borders, and URL/link emphasis.
standouts: The card combines visual swatches with explicit usage guidance.
The central gold tier is marked with a star in the visible label.
Several tier names encode interaction or usage roles such as dashed, hover, and press.
conventions: Uses a metadata HTML comment to describe the design-system card.
Keeps preview-specific styling in an inline style block.
Uses a six-column CSS grid for the scale.
Displays each token as a chip, semantic name, and monospace hex value.
concerns: The six-column grid has no file-local responsive fallback for narrower widths.
Color values are hardcoded directly in inline style attributes rather than referenced through CSS custom properties in the swatches.
notes: A compact preview card documenting the gold accent scale and its intended UI emphasis roles.
questions: Is "gold ★" meant to correspond exactly to --accent-gold?
Should the hardcoded swatch values mirror named design tokens elsewhere?
Is the six-column layout intended only for the declared 700x200 card viewport?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it presents a primary gold accent scale with six tiers and guidance for reserved emphasis use.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make the gold accent palette easy to inspect visually while clarifying that the main gold should be used sparingly for prominent UI emphasis.
novelty: The visible star marker on the main gold tier distinguishes it from the surrounding scale labels.
contribution: A defined gold accent ramp with labeled tonal and state-oriented tiers, hex values, and concise guidance for restrained brand emphasis use.
summary_for_embedding: HTML design-system preview card showing a six-tier gold brand accent palette with swatches, labels, hex values, and usage guidance.
intention_for_embedding: This was for documenting and previewing the gold accent scale as a restrained brand emphasis color set for actions, active borders, and links.
